failure_signature: ACB hospital station unable to induct after robot shutdown state and AGV/tote recovery actions
symptom_summary: Case 223554 at UPS Fort Worth (Haslet) involved the ACB hospital station being unable to induct totes while a robot remained in shutdown state and could not return to normal operation. Source records show the case was opened around 2:11-2:12 AM on 2025-09-26, escalated through CBRE/work order handling, and later documented as resolved after support actions. Teams evidence indicates operators could remove totes but could not add totes at the hospital, with AGVs observed under the rack and at least one AGV showing a disconnected tote association. Recovery actions included removing AGVs 1542188 and 1555028, using API dog to induct a tote to move the system forward, removing tote 51 associated with AGV 1555012 via API dog when hospital removal failed, and re-adding robots. Salesforce closure text states resolution details were to reset Optisweep services and have site restart the Hospital Lane. Root cause is not validated; candidate interpretations include stale AGV/tote association state after AGV removal, robot shutdown state requiring service reset, and recovery complications when tipper stations were not paused.

failure_signature: AGVs stopped during normal operation with no visible faults after OptiSweep/WCS web application fault and out-of-sync AGV states
symptom_summary: Case 228086 at UPS Haslet, TX is a CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure candidate incident. During normal operation on 2026-02-20, AGVs stopped moving with no visible faults and the system still showed standard operation mode. Teams evidence reported 4-6 bots not moving, inability to remove totes from the hospital, question marks on the HMI, and no failed AGVs. Source category context states the web application faulted at 6:36 AM due to memory usage, causing a service crash and AGVs to lose sync. Troubleshooting included remote access, review of Ignition memory trend and uptime, restart of the OptiSweep/Ignition service, review of AGV status and API responses, and correction of out-of-sync AGVs in RMS. Recovery completed the same day, with the site resuming auto bag-out, but infrastructure follow-up was still needed for memory and log sizing.

failure_signature: AGV movement stopped mid-sort with tipper heartbeat failure and hospital tote removal hang
symptom_summary: UPS Haslet CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure incident where AGVs stopped mid-sort, no active alarms were visible on RMS/operator stations, tippers showed heartbeat problems, AVEVA showed tippers closed while field state was open, hospital tote removal hung on processing, remote recovery was delayed by UPS ZScaler VPN outage, and service restart later restored operation. RCA evidence also showed WCSWebApplication database timeout and OPC/OPC-UA communication-related errors, but these remained candidate contributing signals rather than validated root cause.
source_notes: UPS Haslet CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure incident where AGVs stopped mid-sort, no active alarms were visible on RMS/operator stations, tippers showed heartbeat problems, AVEVA showed tippers closed while field state was open, hospital tote removal hung on processing, remote recovery was delayed by UPS ZScaler VPN outage, and service restart later restored operation. RCA evidence also showed WCSWebApplication database timeout and OPC/OPC-UA communication-related errors, but these remained candidate contributing signals rather than validated root cause.

failure_signature: AGVs stopped with no visible fault; Optisweep Windows service restart restored production while root cause investigation continued
symptom_summary: Case 229488 at UPS Haslet, TX was a CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure where AGVs were not moving and no fault was reported. Teams and Salesforce evidence show the issue was treated as a recurrence of Saturday case 229374. Early checks reported tippers mostly in run position, heartbeat sitting flat at 550, and no HMI system-level errors. The case was escalated to software support, and the Optisweep Windows service was restarted remotely around 9:27-9:30 AM. The site then started up and returned to production. Later investigation captured OPC UA connection resets involving hospital controller 10.27.80.157, GLD1 10.27.80.248, and OP station 3 10.27.80.156, plus prior-night tipper alarms including RIO Module Ethernet Failure, ClearLink module not communicating, VFD not detected, and IO Link Master not detected. Recovery after restart was observed, but the source does not validate a final root cause for case 229488.

failure_signature: AGV flow stopped at all three tipper lines with heartbeat timeout and hospital tote removal blocked
symptom_summary: Case 229716 at UPS Haslet, TX matches CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure pattern. Sort started normally, then as AGVs lined up to go to tippers all three lines stopped. Nothing was coming to the tippers, all tippers showed heartbeat timeout while status remained active/enabled, and no faults were visible on the RMS screen. Hospital tote removal would not execute until recovery actions were taken. L2 remotely connected, reviewed heartbeat/alarm views, and engineer guidance directed an E-stop of AGVs, Optisweep service restart, E-stop removal, and possible tipper pause/unpause. After restart, AGVs resumed movement and hospital tote removal recovered after a hospital log out/in. Ignition and Windows event logs were saved, and switch log follow-up was requested. Source states same pattern as prior incidents, but exact initiating OT-side cause was not re-isolated, so root cause remains unvalidated.

failure_signature: Startup state stalled with no path to tippers and recurring tipper heartbeat failure pattern
symptom_summary: Case 229777 at UPS Haslet, TX is a CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure candidate incident. The system was put to startup and everything was waiting, with no path shown and nothing reaching the tippers. Salesforce and Teams evidence indicate the engineer dialed into the system, observed that the tipper heartbeat had failed in the same pattern as case 229716, had the client place the master E-stop on, restarted Optisweep, and paused/unpaused the tippers. The system then came back up and running and the client agreed the case could be closed. This is a recurrence pattern, but validated root cause is not established in this case package.
